message:
fix(core): propagate collation through UNIQUE constraint machinery

Plumb collation metadata through every code path that builds an
in-memory UNIQUE constraint so duplicate detection matches SQLite
under non-default collations (NOCASE, RTRIM, custom).  Before this
commit, UNIQUE enforcement used raw PartialEq on SqliteValue, which
meant 'Foo' and 'foo' were considered distinct by FrankenSQLite even
when the column (or the unique index term) declared COLLATE NOCASE.
That was a silent correctness bug: INSERTs that should have
conflicted went through, and the on-disk compat path round-tripped
to a database SQLite would reject.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs  (~948 line delta)

- infer_create_table_as_select_column_infos(): replace the
  ad-hoc infer_select_column_names + schema-aware CTE resolver
  with the existing select_result_column_names() helper, which
  already handles CTEs, aliases, and schema lookup consistently
  with the rest of the query path.  Fixes a latent mismatch where
  CTAS targets declared different column names than the underlying
  SELECT for WITH-recursive sources.

- INSERT unique-column enforcement:
    - Snapshot the collation registry once at the top of the
      constraint check via collation_registry.lock() so every
      per-row comparison can see custom collations without
      re-locking.
    - Switch column-level UNIQUE checks from `existing_val == new_val`
      to `cmp_sqlite_values_collated_snapshot(existing, new,
      col_info.collation.as_deref(), &coll_snapshot) == Equal`.
    - Change conflicting_rowids from Vec<i64> to
      std::collections::BTreeSet<i64> so ON CONFLICT REPLACE
      deduplicates the replace-target set when the same row matches
      multiple unique groups.

- INSERT unique-index enforcement:
    - Replace the direct `existing_key.iter().zip(new_key).all(|e, n| e.unwrap() == *n)`
      comparison with a per-term loop that looks up the index
      term's collation and uses cmp_sqlite_values_collated_snapshot
      for each column, so composite unique indexes with mixed
      collations compare each position correctly.

- Table-level UNIQUE constraint collection (both the initial
  CREATE TABLE path and the schema-reload path):
    - Replace the filter_map-over-normalize_indexed_column_term
      construction with collect_effective_index_terms_for_column_infos(),
      which returns Some(Vec<IndexTerm { column_name, collation,
      direction }>) only if every term resolves cleanly against
      column_infos (previously a missing column silently dropped
      terms, producing under-wide unique keys).
    - Thread the collations vec through to
      mem_table.add_unique_column_group_with_collations() instead
      of calling the old add_unique_column_group(indices) overload.
      Call this out: add_unique_column_group() still exists on
      MemTable as a thin wrapper that passes None collations for
      any callers that haven't been updated, so this commit only
      moves core to the collation-aware form.

- Implicit column collation for CREATE INDEX:
    - When parsing an indexed-column term in CREATE INDEX, if the
      term didn't explicitly specify COLLATE, fill in
      normalized.collation via
      column_info_collation_by_name(&table.columns, &name) before
      appending it to the collations vec.  That way an index on a
      COLLATE NOCASE column inherits NOCASE without requiring the
      user to re-specify it on the index.

- Schema-reload path (the CREATE TABLE variant that runs during
  VACUUM INTO / ATTACH schema load):
    - new_schema is now typed explicitly as Vec<TableSchema> so the
      follow-up .find/map call can return a Vec<ColumnInfo> for
      use by parse_index_definition_from_create_sql.
    - When reconstructing an index whose owning table isn't yet in
      new_schema, fall back to scanning master_entries for a
      matching "table" row and parsing its CREATE SQL via
      crate::compat_persist::parse_columns_from_sqlite_master_sql.
      Without this fallback, parse_index_definition_from_create_sql
      received an empty columns slice and dropped collations for
      the index terms on the first table encountered during reload.
    - After that, apply the same
      add_unique_column_group_with_collations() plumbing as the
      fresh-CREATE-TABLE path.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/compat_persist.rs

- load_from_sqlite() unique-group harvest (sibling of the
  connection.rs schema-reload path above):
    - Change unique_groups from Vec<Vec<usize>> to
      Vec<(Vec<usize>, Vec<Option<String>>)> so each group carries
      its per-column collation alongside the column index list.
    - Column-level UNIQUE rows now push
      `(vec![column_index], vec![column.collation.clone()])`.
    - For index-level UNIQUE rows, unzip the enumerate().filter_map()
      into (group, collations) pairs and source the collation from
      index.key_collations.get(term_idx).cloned().flatten() so an
      index created with explicit COLLATE preserves the choice.
    - The duplicate-group dedup predicate becomes
      `unique_groups.iter().any(|(existing, _)| existing == &group)`.
    - Final materialization calls
      mem_table.add_unique_column_group_with_collations(group,
      collations) instead of the legacy add_unique_column_group(group).

Together these changes mean that after this commit:
  CREATE TABLE t (name TEXT COLLATE NOCASE UNIQUE);
  INSERT INTO t VALUES ('Foo');
  INSERT INTO t VALUES ('foo');  -- now correctly fails with UNIQUE
and the same is true for composite unique indexes, CREATE INDEX
with implicit collation, and the schema-reload / ATTACH /
compat-load paths that rebuild unique groups from sqlite_master.

No new tests in this commit — the behavior is exercised by the
conformance_oracle_ext suite (see the temporal oracle test commit
in this same batch) and by the existing sqlite-parity conformance
corpus, both of which already carry collation-heavy unique
fixtures that this commit is required to pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
